specific_mechanism: Trilingual cross-check: monolingual hypothesis (Old Persian) tested against parallel inscriptions in two other languages (Elamite, Babylonian). Each language's deciphered substring cascades constraints onto the others; convergence ratified by blind multi-scholar verification (1857 Royal Asiatic test).
llm_application: Trilingual-tool cascade: route the same factual question through 3 distinct tools/agents; require pairwise embedding agreement at termination, halting only when all 3-of-3 outputs agree above a threshold. Each tool's intermediate output narrows the next tool's argument space.
novelty_claim: Multi-tool reasoning literature has chain-of-tools (CoTools), DFSDT tree search, and multi-agent debate, but the 'Behistun 3-of-3 blind agreement' termination criterion specifically is not (yet) called out as a named rule. Whether it is functionally identical to existing multi-agent debate / self-consistency is the open question.
